input.prompt: Why are my Monstera leaves yellowing?

cases.reference_output: Yellowing Monstera leaves are commonly linked to overwatering, poor drainage, low light or normal aging. Check soil moisture and roots before changing care.

retrieved_documents.text: Monstera yellowing can indicate excess water or poor drainage.
Bright indirect light supports Monstera growth.

input.prompt: How often should I water a snake plant?

cases.reference_output: Water snake plants only after the potting mix dries fully. They tolerate drought and are harmed by frequent watering.

retrieved_documents.text: Dracaena trifasciata prefers drying between waterings.

input.prompt: Is direct afternoon sun safe for Calathea?

cases.reference_output: Calathea generally prefers bright filtered light. Direct afternoon sun can scorch leaves and should be avoided.

retrieved_documents.text: Calathea leaves scorch under harsh direct sun.

input.prompt: What causes brown tips on peace lily?

cases.reference_output: Brown tips on peace lily may come from inconsistent watering, low humidity, fertilizer salts or water quality. Trim damage and adjust care gradually.

retrieved_documents.text: Spathiphyllum brown tips can follow water stress or salt accumulation.

input.prompt: Can pothos grow in low light?

cases.reference_output: Pothos can tolerate low light but grows better in bright indirect light. Variegation may fade when light is too low.

retrieved_documents.text: Epipremnum aureum tolerates low light but prefers bright indirect light.

input.prompt: Should I mist my cactus daily?

cases.reference_output: Do not mist a cactus daily. Most cacti prefer dry air, strong light and infrequent deep watering when the mix is dry.

retrieved_documents.text: Cacti are drought-adapted and should not be kept constantly moist.

cases.reference_output: Indoor basil wilts from underwatering, root stress, low light, cold drafts or heat stress. Check soil moisture and provide bright light.

retrieved_documents.text: Basil wilting can result from water stress or insufficient light.

input.prompt: Can I use neem oil on every plant?

cases.reference_output: Neem oil is not suitable for every situation. Test a small leaf area, avoid stressed plants and follow label directions.

retrieved_documents.text: Horticultural oils can damage sensitive or stressed plants.

cases.reference_output: Monstera deliciosa profile: bright indirect light, airy well-drained mix, water when the top layer dries, support climbing growth.

retrieved_documents.text: Monstera deliciosa is a climbing aroid that prefers bright indirect light.

cases.reference_output: Dracaena trifasciata profile: drought tolerant, low to bright indirect light, water sparingly and use fast-draining substrate.

retrieved_documents.text: Dracaena trifasciata is drought tolerant and dislikes saturated soil.

cases.reference_output: Spathiphyllum wallisii profile: medium indirect light, evenly moist but not soggy soil, higher humidity and caution around pets.

retrieved_documents.text: Peace lily prefers evenly moist substrate and indirect light.

cases.reference_output: Epipremnum aureum profile: adaptable vine, bright indirect light for best variegation, water after partial drying.

retrieved_documents.text: Pothos tolerates varied indoor light and should dry slightly between waterings.

cases.reference_output: Ficus lyrata profile: bright filtered light, stable placement, thorough watering after partial drying and careful acclimation.

retrieved_documents.text: Fiddle-leaf fig prefers bright light and consistent care.

cases.reference_output: Calathea orbifolia profile: filtered light, evenly moist soil, warm temperatures and high humidity without direct sun.

retrieved_documents.text: Calathea orbifolia needs humidity and filtered light.

cases.reference_output: Aloe vera profile: bright light, gritty well-draining mix and infrequent watering after the soil dries completely.

retrieved_documents.text: Aloe vera is a succulent requiring bright light and dry intervals.

Move it to filtered light, remove only fully dead tissue, keep moisture steady and avoid fertilizer until new growth appears.
Water thoroughly, drain excess water, monitor recovery over several hours and adjust to consistently moist soil without waterlogging.

input.prompt: Remind me to repot snake plant next spring

cases.reference_output: Create a repotting reminder for the snake plant next spring with a clear due date or ask for one if needed.
80 lux is very low for pothos. Move it closer to a bright window or add grow light support gradually.
900 lux is modest for Monstera. It may survive but brighter indirect light is likely better for strong growth.
12000 lux can suit many cacti if acclimated. Watch for scorching and increase exposure gradually.
Direct afternoon sun is risky for Calathea. Prefer filtered bright light and monitor leaf scorch.
500 lux is low but may be tolerated by peace lily. Flowering and growth improve with brighter indirect light.
700 lux is too low for productive basil. Provide much brighter light or a grow light.
2500 lux is moderate for aloe. Brighter light may improve compact growth if the plant is acclimated.
